hypothesis: If crystal structures are perturbed by realistic lattice and coordinate noise, then gradient-descent optimization of differentiable XRD-similarity losses (negative cosine similarity, MSE, or negative entropy similarity) will not reliably recover the ground-truth structures because the XRD-based loss landscape is highly nonconvex and rough with many local minima, and enforcing the correct space-group symmetry via projected gradient descent does not substantially improve recovery relative to energy-based relaxation using the CHGNet potential.
